titleStmt.title: Letter from the Earl of Shaftsbury to the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland, 31 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Anthony Ashley-Cooper

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from the Earl of Shaftsbury (1869-1961), H.M. Lieutenant of Co. Antrim to Lord Wimborne (1873-1939), Lord Lieutenant for Ireland, concerning the appointment of Deputy Lieutenants for County Antrim. He suggests four names of gentlemen to be appointed as deputy lieutenants for Co Antrim.Anthony Ashley Cooper (1869-1961), 9th Earl of Shaftsbury, served as Lord Lieutenant of Antrim from 1911 to 1916, Chancellor of Queen's University, and Lord Lieutenant of Dorset from 1916 to 1952. Cooper retired from the British Regular Army in 1899, but would continue to serve as captain, and later lieutenant colonel of the North of Ireland Imperial Yeomanry. During the Great War, Cooper held the temporary rank of brigadier-general, and was subsequently honoured with the rank in 1919. Ivor Churchill Guest, 1st Viscount Wimborne was appointed the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland in 1915. Guest worked closely alongside Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) in the administration of Ireland. Following the suppression of the Rising, Guest, under pressure, was forced to resign as Lord Lieutenant. A later inquiry into the Rising exonerated Guest and held Augustine Birrell (1850-1933) chiefly responsible for the events that occurred. Guest would continue to serve as Lord Lieutenant until 1918.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Belfast Castle. 31.XII.15. His Excellency. The Lord Lieutenant of Ireland. Your Excellency , I desire to bring forward the
names of the following Gentlemen for
appointment as Deputy Lieutenants in the
County of Antrim
I. Stouppe Mc. Cauce Esq.
of Woodbourne.
Dunmurry Co Antrim.
Edward Coey Esq.
of Merville
Belfast.
Roger Casement Esq.
of Magherintemple
Ballycastle Co Antrim.
Captain John Patrick
of Dunminning
Glarryford Co Antrim.
All the above are qualified for the
appointment. Have served or are serving their
Country in same capacity or other whilst two of
them have sons serving in H. M. Army & Navy. I have the honour to be
Your Excellency's obedient servant . Shaftesbury.
H. M. L.
of the Co. of Antrim.
